mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance or negative mean during rate cuts across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of co-expansion periods during walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias or negative mean following positive GDP revisions across a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly with p-value above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or interaction effects vanish when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO trends fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a positive Rank IC exceeding 0.04 in walk-forward tests.
